input.prompt:
I have two CLR activation logs for the same tool (mt.exe). When I run
the build from my script, it works fine. When I run MSBuild directly
from cmd.exe, I get FOD dialogs. Both logs show the same DllGetClassObject
activation for the same CLSID. Why is the behavior different?

data.content:

the build from my script, it works fine. When I run MSBuild directly
from cmd.exe, I get FOD dialogs. Both logs show the same DllGetClassObject
activation for the same CLSID. Why is the behavior different?

data.content:
The logs are nearly identical except for one critical difference:

**Line 20 — `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS`:**

| Log | Value | Result (line 37) |
|---|---|---|
| `mt-from-cmdline` | **0** | `Launching feature-on-demand installation…` |
| `mt-from-script` | **32769** (0x8001) | `Could have launched…if was not opted out` |

The value `32769` = `0x8001` = `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS (0x1)` | `SEM_NOGPFAULTERRORBOX (0x8000)`. Your script (or the process tree it runs under) calls `SetErrorMode` with `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS`, which tells the CLR activation shim (`mscoreei.dll`) to **suppress** the FOD/fondue.exe dialog. A plain `cmd.exe` session has error mode `0`, so the shim launches `fondue.exe` to install .NET Framework 3.5.

**The root cause:** mt.exe requires **.NET Framework v2.0/3.5** (the log settles on `v2.0.50727`), which isn't installed. Both paths fail to find a runtime — the only difference is whether the error triggers a UI dialog or is silently swallowed.

**To fix:** Either install .NET 3.5 (`DISM /Online /Enable-Feature /FeatureName:NetFx3`) or, if mt.exe doesn't actually need the CLR COM object, investigate why that CLSID is being activated.
data.reasoningText:
Let me compare the two logs carefully.

The key difference is on line 20:

**cmdline log (FOD dialog):** `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set to 0`
**script log (no FOD dialog):** `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set to 32769`

And then:

**cmdline log (line 37):** `Launching feature-on-demand installation. CmdLine: "C:\WINDOWS\system32\fondue.exe" /enable-feature:NetFx3 /caller-name:mscoreei.dll`


**script log (line 37):** `Could have launched feature-on-demand installation if was not opted out. CmdLine: "C:\WINDOWS\system32\fondue.exe" /enable-feature:NetFx3 /caller-name:mscoreei.dll`

So the critical difference is that `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS` flag value of 32769 in the script case actually combines two error handling flags: `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS` (0x0001) and `SEM_NOGPFAULTERRORBOX` (0x8000), which together equal 0x8001 or 32769 in decimal.

When this flag is set, the CLR activation shim treats the process as having opted out of critical error handling, so it silently skips the FOD installation dialog instead of launching fondue.exe like it does when the flag is 0. The script or MSBuild likely calls `SetErrorMode` with this combined flag value internally.
